description: Catch-up currently upserts observed segments but can retain stale/missing segment rows. Make reconciliation set-total with explicit deletions/presence states so stale durable segment topology is unrepresentable.
title: P0: Reconcile WAL segment index as total set (upsert + delete)

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `bd migrate detect` reports structural status (deps format, notes/checksums presence, reasons).
- [ ] `bd migrate to 1` rewrites legacy deps stores to strict OR-Set v1 with safe ref update semantics.
- [ ] Runtime strict load path does not gain backward-compat fallback.
- [ ] Checkpoint incompatibility is classified and self-heals via rebuild.
- [ ] Tests and migration docs cover rollout and edge cases.
description:
**Problem**
Strict runtime parsing now expects `WireDepStoreV1` in `deps.jsonl`, but some real store refs still use legacy line-per-edge deps and fail with missing `cc`, bricking store-load commands.

Hard-cutover policy requires explicit migration rather than runtime compatibility parsing.

**Files**: docs/plans/2026-03-01-legacy-deps-hard-cutover-design.md
design:
**Design**
Execute the refined hard-cutover plan in scoped slices: CLI/backend contracts, detect classifier, migration-only wire/sync loaders, migrate executor with retry safety, checkpoint incompatible-classification and rebuild behavior, reuse hardening, runtime hinting, and migration docs/rollout guidance.

Keep runtime canonical load strict; confine legacy deps parsing to detect/migrate paths only.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Audit on 2026-03-11 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover @ 03adab51: slice .8 now documents the operator rollout/rollback recipe and slice .10 now adds realistic checked-in fixture coverage beyond the minimal v0.1.26 boundary. With .1 through .10 now closed, the hard-cutover epic acceptance is fully covered in code, tests, and migration docs.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Audit on 2026-03-11 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover @ d7dbb2dc and 9aacf74f: follow-up slices .16 through .20 are now landed and closed. This pass aligned detect/dry-run source resolution with execute, preserved unrelated-history safety and true no-op convergence across retries, inferred missing `root_slug` from canonical state, corrected rollout/rollback docs for converged and mixed-version fleets, and tightened adversarial migration coverage around ancestry, note identity, and checksum edge cases. Remaining open work is outside the epic scope: `beads-rs-qin2` tracks sourcing an independently captured legacy deleted-dep fixture, and `beads-rs-iymc` tracks the unrelated slow-test Unix-socket path-length failure.

title: Legacy deps hard cutover to OR-Set v1

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] hash_prev_machine.rs updated or a new model added (e.g., canonical_hash_machine.rs) and compiles
- [ ] Properties cover: canonical-only acceptance, unknown-key preservation for hash validity, prev continuity per §0.12.1, and defined behavior for out-of-order events
- [ ] Model doc comments reference REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.6, §0.6.1, §0.12.1, and §9.4
- [ ] Example runs via cargo run --example canonical_hash_machine (or equivalent)
description:
**Problem**
The hash/prev continuity and canonical CBOR rules (REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.6, §0.6.1, §0.12.1, §9.4) are foundational. We have hash_prev_machine.rs, but it does not cover unknown-key preservation or the out-of-order buffering vs prev-check policy implied by §9.4.

**Design Notes**
This model should clarify how verification interacts with gaps and how canonicalness is enforced before WAL append.
design:
**Design**
Extend hash_prev_machine.rs or create a new model that adds: (a) a canonical/ non-canonical flag for incoming bytes, (b) unknown-key preservation as a requirement for hash verification, and (c) explicit policy for out-of-order events (buffered until prev is known vs immediate rejection). Tie that policy to Plan §9.4 and §0.12.1 in comments and pick the interpretation the plan intends.

Model invariants: wrong store => reject, sha mismatch => reject, non-canonical => reject, prev mismatch => reject, equivocation => reject. Ensure contiguity is only advanced after successful verification and (if buffered) after gaps are filled.
title: Stateright: canonical hash/prev gating + unknown-key rules
